description: Labeled dataset for memory classification eval. Samples picked from eval/slack_raw.json and manually labeled.

notes.channel_kind: Confirmed channel type (override guessed_kind from slack_raw.json if needed)
notes.project_slug: Fill in if channel maps to a project (from channel name convention)
notes.expected_action: What the agent SHOULD do with this message
notes.target_scope: Where the data SHOULD be stored (none if action=ignore or requires_human_confirmation)
notes.safety_class: safe=can go anywhere, internal_only=team only never client, client_safe=can be shown to client, private=user only, sensitive=needs human review, forbidden=must never be stored
notes.fact_kind: Only fill if expected_action=create_fact or propose_fact
notes.requires_review: true if a human must confirm before any action
notes.reason: Short explanation of why this label is correct — especially important for sensitive/forbidden

critical_labels.false_positive_risk: Any sample where expected_action != create_memory but LLM might want to create_memory for a public_client scope is a CRITICAL false positive. Flag these.
critical_labels.forbidden_in_public_client: Messages that are internal_only or sensitive in a project_public_client channel — the only valid actions are ignore or requires_human_confirmation, NEVER create_memory/create_fact directly.

samples._comment: TEMPLATE — copy this block for each sample, remove _comment field
samples.reason: Krótkie uzasadnienie dlaczego ta etykieta jest poprawna
